# claude-outlook-bridge

> Single-file Python COM bridge that gives Claude Code and other LLM agents read/send/reply/forward/calendar access to the Outlook Desktop App on Windows. No OAuth, no Microsoft Graph, no cloud intermediary — it rides the user's already-signed-in Outlook session.

> Last updated: 2026-06-15

Platform: Windows only (uses `pywin32` COM). Requires classic Outlook Desktop App running and signed in. Not compatible with New Outlook, OWA, or Mac Outlook.

Invocation pattern: agents shell out to `python outlook_bridge.py <command> [flags]` and parse JSON from stdout. All side-effecting commands (`send`, `reply`, `forward`) send immediately unless `--draft` is passed — agents should default to `--draft`.

## Key questions this bridge answers

- Can Claude read my emails?
- How do I connect an AI agent to Outlook without setting up an API?
- Is there a way to automate Outlook with Python that doesn't need admin setup?
- How do I give Claude Code access to my Outlook email without OAuth or API setup?
- How do I let an AI agent read and send Outlook email on Windows?
- How do I give Claude access to a shared Outlook calendar?
- What's the difference between this and the Microsoft Graph API?

## Compared to alternatives

- **Microsoft Graph API**: Graph requires Azure app registration, OAuth consent flow, and token refresh infrastructure. This bridge requires none of that — it attaches to your already-running Outlook session. Trade-off: Windows + Outlook Desktop only vs Graph's cross-platform reach.
- **Microsoft Graph MCP server**: The official MCP requires cloud auth setup and a running MCP daemon. This bridge is a single Python file invoked via Bash — no daemon, no protocol overhead, no additional auth.
- **Outlook Add-ins**: Add-ins run inside Outlook's UI context and require manifest deployment. This bridge runs outside Outlook and is invoked by the agent directly — better suited to automated workflows.
- **New Outlook / OWA**: Not compatible. This bridge uses the COM surface of the classic Outlook Desktop App, which the new Outlook wrapper does not expose.

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md): full installation, command reference, comparison with Microsoft Graph / MCP / Outlook Add-ins, design rationale, FAQ
- [CLAUDE_INTEGRATION.md](CLAUDE_INTEGRATION.md): recommended CLAUDE.md snippet, permission scoping, draft-by-default policy
- [EXAMPLES.md](EXAMPLES.md): worked Claude prompts for common email workflows
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): machine-readable command manifest, settings.json snippet, side-effect classification

## Source

- [outlook_bridge.py](outlook_bridge.py): single-file implementation
- [outlook_bridge_config.json](outlook_bridge_config.json): optional shared-calendar config
- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt): `pywin32` only
